reasoning: Paul Biedermann's 200m freestyle world record of 1:42.00 was set in 2009 during the super-suit era and is considered one of the hardest swimming records to break. Since the banning of these suits, no swimmer has matched the time—David Popovici came the closest in 2022 with a time of 1:42.97. The most recent major long-course event, the 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore, saw Popovici win the event with a time of 1:43.53, still more than 1.5 seconds off the record. Furthermore, the forecast window runs from November 2025 to January 2026, which falls squarely into the short-course swimming season. Serious long-course world record attempts during these winter months are exceedingly rare. A retroactive doping disqualification for Biedermann is also effectively impossible given WADA's 10-year statute of limitations for sample re-testing. As such, the probability of the record falling is practically zero, and my estimates are set as close to the 0.95 upper limit as possible, decreasing slightly for more distant dates to appropriately reflect horizon uncertainty.
